reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome; model verified in ORT-web via local load; light+dark, mobile+desktop layout)

rubric.evidence: Model verified to run in ORT-web / transformers.js (headless Chrome): loaded onnx-community/twitter-roberta-large-hate-latest-ONNX q8 and ran AutoModelForSequenceClassification, returning REAL correct labels — 'go back to your own country' → hate_origin:0.988, 'All women are stupid…' → hate_gender:0.997, 'Gay people are disgusting…' → hate_sexuality:0.996, 'Your religion is a disease…' → hate_religion:0.998, benign → not_hate:1.0. Inference runs in a Web Worker. NOTE: the 358 MB q8 file's in-browser HF fetch stalled at ~262/358 MB in THIS sandbox (network cap on the large single file), so the run was verified by serving the identical model files locally (env.localModelPath) — same weights, same runtime; the page itself still loads from HF for users. Page wiring is identical to the fully end-to-end-verified offensive-language-detection page.
rubric.notes: RoBERTa-large (355M), q8, single-label softmax over 8 classes (7 protected-group + not_hate).

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — the eight classes are one softmax summing to ~100%; the model both decides hate (1 − P(not_hate)) and names the targeted group. Verified: explicit protected-group attacks map to the correct hate_* class with high confidence; nasty-but-not-group-targeted text and benign text map to not_hate. Output shape + semantics correct, not fake.
rubric.notes: Conservative model: strong precision on explicit attacks, weaker recall on coded/subtle hate (surfaced honestly in the wild page).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/twitter-roberta-large-hate-latest-ONNX linked to card + base cardiffnlp repo), task (text-classification), backend (WASM), dtype (q8), and the honest ~358 MB size — matches conformance page-text/source assertions.

Uses the shared lib/model-loader.js createModelLoader (no hand-rolled button); fresh profile shows 'Download model (~358 MB)'. The cached-auto-init path is the shared architecture verified end-to-end on the sibling offensive page (identical loader wiring); it could not be re-exercised here because the 358 MB in-browser HF download stalls in this sandbox. transformers.js caches via Cache Storage so a completed download auto-inits on return.
rubric.notes: Large model — the loader shows size and never silently re-downloads.

rubric.evidence: Loader status is a role=status aria-live region (checking → initialising → ready); download progress streams to <progress>; failures surface to a .status.err region with Retry; scoring errors write to #runStatus.err. At-a-glance carries an explicit honest large-download caveat ('heavy for low-memory phones'). Runs on the WASM path (no GPU) — verified real output in ORT-web.

Agent verdict — textarea, four sample chips (min 34px keyboard-operable buttons), hate-threshold slider (re-renders verdict live), and the loader Download/Clear controls all operable across index + basics/practical/wild/multimodel. Sample chips were tuned against the REAL model so each fires its labelled category (origin/gender/religion/sexuality) or correctly clears.

Agent verdict — bars/verdict are produced by the on-device worker run; verified real ORT-web labels (above). No canned result anywhere; on a device that can't fetch/hold the model the loader shows an honest Download/Retry state, never a faked score.

Conformance images-have-alt (no content images) and buttons-have-names pass; probability bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow + aria-label; loader status is role=status aria-live. Design-system contrast (light+dark) inherited from public/styles.css.

Agent matrix pass — desktop 1280×800 and mobile 360×740 DPR3 both automated-clean (no horizontal overflow, network+console clean) and screenshots read: header, at-a-glance table (with the honest size note), chips, slider, bars legible and wrapped; multimodel compare grid collapses to one column on mobile. Screens: reports/responsive/screens/hate-speech-detection/{desktop,mobile}.png. Mobile kept at needs-review: layout is clean but a 358 MB / 355M-param WASM model is memory-heavy for low-end phones and was not exercised on a constrained device.
rubric.notes: Desktop ok; mobile layout ok but heavy — honest caveat recorded.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — all inference (tokenizer → model → softmax) runs in a Web Worker; the batch triage loop runs in the worker so the main thread never blocks; input debounced 350 ms; multimodel runs the heads with Promise.all off-thread. Main thread stayed responsive.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread web worker batch loop
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep heavy synchronous work off the interaction path; break up long loops.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all inference runs in a Web Worker (worker.js); batch triage loops inside the worker; input debounced; multimodel composes engines with Promise.all.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: ORT-web run in-worker; main-thread eval responsive; 0 console errors

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status announcements aria-live; label every control
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/progress/error in a live region; give every control an accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live loader, labelled slider + chips + textarea, probability bars as role=meter with aria-valuenow/aria-label.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel + multi-column compare grid without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use intrinsic sizing + grid with a single-column mobile fallback + min-inline-size:0 so columns can shrink; flex-wrap chips; overflow-wrap for long text.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — compare grid is 1fr on mobile and 1fr 1fr (hate) / repeat(3,1fr) (offensive) above a breakpoint, columns use min-inline-size:0, queue text uses overflow-wrap:anywhere; verified no overflow at 360px and 1280px.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive matrix desktop+mobile no-horizontal-overflow pass; screenshots read

guidanceConsulted.query: cross document view transitions navigation between catalogue and page
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inherited from the shared public/styles.css; crumbs link back to catalogue + model page.

openQuestions: The 358 MB q8 download is heavy for mobile; is a smaller genuine protected-group-hate ONNX available upstream? (base cardiff/facebook/CNERG hate models ship no ONNX today — only this large variant does.)
Recall gap: the model misses coded/subtle/policy-framed hate (verified). Worth pairing with a toxicity/offensiveness head by default (the multimodel demo shows this).

followUpGoals.goal: If a base-size (≈125M) protected-group hate ONNX appears upstream, add it as a lighter additive companion demo (keep this large one).
Add a WebGPU execution-provider fast path for the large hate model (honest WASM fallback retained).

summary: Revision-1 agent build+matrix pass for hate-speech-detection (cardiffnlp/twitter-roberta-large-hate-latest via onnx-community, ~358 MB, WASM q8). The model was verified to run in ORT-web and return correct protected-group labels (hate_origin/gender/religion/sexuality vs not_hate); sample chips tuned against the real model; desktop+mobile layout screenshots read (no overflow, legible), console clean. The in-browser 358 MB HF fetch stalls in this sandbox, so real inference was verified via local model-file loading; the page wiring is identical to the fully end-to-end-verified offensive page. Framed honestly as an imperfect, conservative signal (strong precision, real recall gap) — never a verdict.
